business.headline: No Claim Bonus: The motor insurance reward many drivers lose unknowinglyHidden inside every motor insurance policy is something called No Claim Bonus, or NCB, a reward insurers give customers for not making claims. Over time, it can reduce premiums significantly. Yet despite being one of the most valuable benefits in motor insurance, it is also one of the least understood.

Inflation finds new fuelHandcrafted ByOur Editors India's latest fuel price hike could trigger a wider inflation ripple across the economy, raising concerns over costlier food, deliveries, transport and growing pressure on household budgets.

Govt hikes export duty on petrol to Rs 3 per litre, cuts diesel, ATF levies from May 16The differential taxation strategy is expected to boost government revenue from petroleum exports without affecting price stability for Indian consumers.

Fuel price hike right move, oil firms can't absorb loss for long period: Ashok LahiriIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Dr Ashok Lahiri, the newly appointed Vice-Chairman of NITI Aayog, discussed the recent fuel price hikes, the state of the Indian economy, and the impact of the West Asia conflict.
Sensex, Nifty slip as rupee hits record lowThe benchmark BSE Sensex fell 160.73 points, or 0.21%, to close at 75,237.99, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 46.10 points, or 0.19%, to settle at 23,643.50.

Reliance-Disney's JioStar sues Zee over alleged unauthorised film broadcasts: ReportJioStar has moved against Zee over alleged unauthorised telecasts of 12 Bollywood films. The clash adds to a widening rights battle between two major media companies.

Maharashtra cuts VAT on jet fuel from 18% to 7% amid rising aviation costsFuel is one of the biggest expenses for airlines and accounts for nearly 35-40% of an airline's operating costs. The tax reduction is expected to lower refuelling costs at Mumbai airport, which is India's second-busiest airport after Delhi and handles around 15% of the country's air traffic.

Petrol, diesel prices could rise over next 3-4 months amid crude oil crisisThe Rs 3-per-litre fuel price hike may not be the last, with analysts warning that persistently high crude oil prices could trigger more petrol and diesel price increases over coming three to four months.
Rupee hits all-time low, breaches 96 for 1st time against US dollarThe rupee weakened 0.3% to 96.05 against the dollar, crossing its previous all-time low of 95.9575 touched in the previous session.

HPCL dealers allege pressure to push 'Power Petrol' amid fuel price hikePetrol dealers have alleged that state-run oil firms are pressuring them to prioritise premium fuel orders amid a fresh Rs 3 per litre hike. The claim has added to concerns over consumer choice and retail fuel availability during the West Asia-linked energy crisis.

Beyond the petrol pump: How higher fuel prices could hit India's economyThe Rs 3-per-litre petrol and diesel price hike may only be the start of a new cycle of fuel price increases as crude oil prices remain high, with economists warning of wider pressure on inflation, growth, household budgets and the rupee

Petrol, diesel prices hiked after years. Here's what else could get costlierRising petrol and diesel prices are starting to push up transport and household costs across India. Economists say the impact could spread to food, deliveries, air travel and inflation if crude stays high.
Sensex, Nifty open higher despite rise in fuel prices; IT stocks lead gainsThe S&P BSE Sensex was up 292.55 points to 75,691.27, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 83.70 points to 23,773.30 as of 9:34 am.

Fuel costlier by Rs 3 per litre: Why government raised petrol, diesel pricesThe government and state-run oil companies raised petrol and diesel prices by Rs 3 per litre on Friday after keeping rates unchanged for weeks.

8th Pay Commission: What happened at the Cabinet Secretary-led NCJCM meetingAt the NCJCM meeting on May 11, employee unions placed pay, pension, healthcare and staffing demands before senior government officials. The discussion gained added weight as it ran alongside 8th Pay Commission consultations and prompted directions on several pending issues.

India's fuel crisis could push a long-overdue rethink of how cities movePM Modi's seven appeals are the right signal at the right time. But fuel security and clean air require infrastructure, not just intention, and that demands a place in every state and central budget.

Petrol, diesel prices hiked by Rs 3 nationwide; CNG also up in Delhi by Rs 2  Live UpdatesIndia had so far avoided a major increase in retail fuel prices despite surging global crude oil prices as a result of the West Asian conflict. However, speculation over another round of hikes had been building for days as global crude continued to climb.

Govt caps duty-free gold imports at 100 kg under Advance Authorisation schemeGold imports in India, the world's second-biggest gold consumer after China, are driven by the jewellery industry demand. Such imports involve a substantial outflow of foreign exchange.

US moving to end $265 million fraud cases against Gautam Adani: ReportThe US Justice Department is reportedly set to drop criminal fraud and bribery charges against Gautam Adani linked to an alleged $265 million corruption scheme tied to India's largest solar power project, according to a Bloomberg report.

We asked top banks how they fight digital fraud. None respondedIn the third and final part of the series, Banking Risks in Digital India, IndiaToday.in reached out to major banks to understand how they are preparing for the next generation of digital fraud — from deepfakes and mule accounts to coercion-driven scams and AI-enabled deception. None of the banks responded.

entertainment.headline: Berlin and the Lady with an Ermine review: Pedro Alonso returns with flairRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Returning to the Money Heist universe, Netflix's Berlin and the Lady with an Ermine delivers a lighter, more playful caper that relies heavily on Pedro Alonso's undeniable charm and flair.

Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 trends at No 1 in Pakistan after Netflix release A content creator has claimed Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge reached number one on Netflix in Pakistan after its international release on May 14.

Vijay, Rajinikanth, MGR: India's dangerous love affair with celebrity worship Why does India turn its film stars into gods? From women writing letters in blood for Rajesh Khanna to temples built for Rajinikanth and Vijay, this is the story of how celebrity worship in India blurred the line between fandom, faith and political power.

Trisha Krishnan blushes as fan asks about CM Vijay at Karuppu screening. Video At the Karuppu screening, actor Trisha Krishnan was left blushing as a fan asked her about Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay.

Salman Khan on his film selection process: Never read a script in my entire life Salman Khan has said that he has never read a script in his entire life. He was recently seen in a cameo appearance in Riteish Deshmukh's Marathi film Raja Shivaji.

Pati Patni Aur Woh Do box office Day 1: Ayushmann's sequel behind Kartik's film Ayushmann Khurrana's Pati Patni Aur Woh Do released on May 15 had a modest opening. The film witnessed mixed reviews, low occupancy and a lower start than Kartik Aaryan's 2019 film, Pati Patni Aur Woh.

Kamal Haasan calls for production cuts in Indian cinema amid West Asia crisis Kamal Haasan has asked the Indian film industry to rein in wasteful spending and rethink costly production habits amid rising economic pressure during the West Asia conflict. He said any cost correction must protect workers and push the industry towards more disciplined, sustainable filmmaking.

Kanye West's India debut show cancelled for 2nd time after government directive Kanye West's India debut concert in New Delhi on May 23, 2026, has been cancelled after official security directives. Organisers have promised full refunds and are discussing a fresh date and venue with Kanye's team.

Saif Ali Khan: If you look up Bollywood, you'll see Shah Rukh Khan Saif Ali Khan spoke about Shah Rukh Khan's drive, self-belief and behind-the-scenes rigour, adding that the qualities gave the superstar his enduring stature. Saif's new film, Kartavya, is produced by SRK's banner.

Celebrity hairstylist Aalim Hakim sets rule against stars with ill-mannered managers Celebrity hairstylist Aalim Hakim has announced a major professional decision involving actors and their teams. His statement has sparked fresh conversation about respect behind the scenes in the film industry.

Hokum review: Adam Scott's film is creepy, clever… then completely lostRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Hokum review: Adam Scott plays a troubled horror writer who reaches a remote Irish hotel to scatter his parents' ashes, and becomes fixated on a locked honeymoon suite. Damian McCarthy's film sustains an eerie folklore mood and strong performances, but the narrative grows increasingly uneven.

Shehnaaz Gill dating cricketer Devdutt Padikkal? IPL appearances spark buzz Shehnaaz Gill's repeated appearances at IPL matches featuring Royal Challengers Bengaluru, and her remarks about favourite players, have sparked dating rumours linking her to cricketer Devdutt Padikkal.

Exam review: A promising scam thriller that turns predictableRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Exam review: Director Sarkunam's Exam, starring Dushara Vijayan and Aditi Balan, is a show about impersonation and India's civil service exam paper leak. The series starts on a promising note but fizzles midway.

Prince Harry, Meghan Markle adapt Afghan war memoir No Way Out for Netflix film Prince Harry and Meghan Markle are turning Major Adam Jowett's memoir into a Netflix feature film. The project connects closely to Harry's own military past and marks a new chapter for their production house.

John Abraham heads to Cannes with Amma Ariyan, but not the one you think A restored Malayalam classic titled Amma Ariyan is heading to Cannes Classics, but the John Abraham behind it is not the Bollywood actor many expect.

Matt Reeves confirms Scarlett Johansson, Sebastian Stan in The Batman: Part II Matt Reeves has unveiled a new cast for The Batman Part II. Along with Robert Pattinson in the title role, the film will star Scarlett Johansson, Sebastian Stan and Charles Dance. The new adventure set in Gotham City promises to be colder, as it tells a story of corruption and family scars.

Fashion first, cinema later: How Cannes became India's favourite runway From couture chaos to celebrity frenzy, Cannes 2026 has reignited an old debate in India. Is the world's most prestigious film festival still about cinema, or has the red carpet stolen the spotlight completely?

JioStar takes legal action against Zee over unauthorised telecast of Bollywood films In a 120-page plea, JioStar alleged that Zee Entertainment Enterprises broadcast 12 distinct films nearly 20 times, including several blockbusters starring Shah Rukh Khan and Aamir Khan.

general news.headline: CBI arrests 'mastermind' Pune biology teacher in NEET-UG paper leak caseThe arrest was made on the basis of questioning of alleged kingpin PV Kulkarni, a retired Chemistry professor, and other accused in the paper leak case, officials said.

Totally baseless, did not criticise youth: CJI on 'jobless cockroaches' remarkChief Justice of India Surya Kant clarified that his "cockroaches" remark was wrongly projected as criticism of the youth, saying he had actually targeted people using fake degrees to enter professions like law and media.

Host Olympics, become world's growth engine: PM Modi's 4 ambitions for IndiaPM Modi said that India wants to host the Olympics, become a global manufacturing and green energy hub, and drive the world's economic growth. During his visit to the Netherlands, Modi is scheduled to hold talks with Dutch Prime Minister Rob Jetten and meet King Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima.

Why Trump team dumped Chinese gifts, badges before leaving BeijingThe US delegation discarded every item handed out by Chinese officials before boarding Air Force One in Beijing. The directive was absolute - no item of Chinese origin was permitted in the aircraft.

The real NEET crisis? Parents who believe any price is worth an MBBS seatHandcrafted ByOur Editors The NEET scandal has exposed more than just leaked papers and broken exam systems. It has revealed the terrifying desperation inside India's middle-class homes, where becoming a doctor is no longer a dream, but a family survival project.

Choose geography or history: Army chief's stern warning to PakistanArmy Chief General Upendra Dwivedi warned Pakistan against sheltering terrorists and targeting India. His remarks signalled a hard line if any fresh provocation comes after last year's Operation Sindoor.

Operation Epic Fury 2.0 soon? US planning big strikes on Iran after China visitHowever, Donald Trump faces a difficult political calculation ahead of the mid-term polls in November. The war is increasingly unpopular, and its financial cost is being felt by ordinary Americans. Allies have been pressing Trump to reach a compromise that would prompt Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and allow him to declare victory.

Darling, leaked papers for you: Professor sends obscene texts to student, arrestedThe student reportedly suffered severe mental distress and informed the Lucknow University administration about the matter, following which Lucknow University lodged a formal complaint with the police.

Vijay, Rajinikanth, MGR: India's dangerous love affair with celebrity worshipWhy does India turn its film stars into gods? From women writing letters in blood for Rajesh Khanna to temples built for Rajinikanth and Vijay, this is the story of how celebrity worship in India blurred the line between fandom, faith and political power.

Why Xi took Trump on a rare tour to Zhongnanhai, China's most secretive power hubChinese President Xi Jinping took US President Donald Trump inside Zhongnanhai, China's tightly guarded Communist Party leadership compound, after talks on Taiwan, trade, Iran and Indo-Pacific tensions. The Zhongnanhai trip was significant against the backdrop of Trump's first China visit in nearly nine years.

5 scuba divers went 164 feet deep into a Maldives cave and never resurfacedThe five divers began their expedition to explore underwater caves in the Vaavu Atoll, located at a depth of 50 metres, but never resurfaced. The cause of their deaths is under investigation. Recovery operations are expected to resume on Saturday.

Has jhalmuri reached here too: PM Modi's Bengal election quip in NetherlandsWhile the PM praised voter participation in recent Assembly polls, cheering supporters interrupted him, prompting a smiling quip: "Jhalmuri yahan bhi pahoch gayi?", drawing laughter at the event in The Hague.

Trisha Krishnan blushes as fan asks about CM Vijay at Karuppu screening. VideoAt the Karuppu screening, actor Trisha Krishnan was left blushing as a fan asked her about Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay.
The dual-use Great Nicobar Island ProjectIndia's most significant port project is a geopolitical project of enormous ramifications.

Will no longer terrorise us: Trump says Islamic State's No. 2 killed in daring opWith the death of Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command of the Islamic State, the global operations of the terror outfit are now greatly diminished, US President Donald Trump said.

ICC meeting in Ahmedabad during IPL final. Will Pak chief Mohsin Naqvi attend?Following the relocation of the ICC meetings to Ahmedabad, PCB Chairman Mohsin Naqvi's travel to India remains highly uncertain despite reports claiming he has been invited to the IPL 2026 final.

Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 trends at No 1 in Pakistan after Netflix releaseA content creator has claimed Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge reached number one on Netflix in Pakistan after its international release on May 14.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Made-in-India C295 aircraft to fly out soon; used by US to rescue pilots from IranThe first India-made C295 is expected to be rolled out from Tata's Vadodara facility soon. The production of the aircraft marks a major shift in India's defence manufacturing ecosystem, breaking HAL's decades-long monopoly and signalling the rise of private-sector military aircraft production.

The viral father-son duo reflecting Tamil Nadu's political shiftKartik belongs to the generation that has rallied behind actor-turned-politician Vijay and his party, Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK). His father, meanwhile, remains deeply loyal to the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) and the Dravidian movement.

You don't appreciate India until you can't get onions in 10 minutesHandcrafted ByOur Editors While India may be falling short on several fronts, one thing it gets undeniably right is convenience. From instant house help to late-night food delivery, the ease and affordability of these services are hard to match abroad where they exist, but come at a steep price.

Watch: Unending lighting streak seen in the US. What was behind it?On the night of May 15, 2026, a massive storm system produced near-continuous lightning across Nebraska and Iowa, sending the internet into shock. Scientists explain the extraordinary physics behind one of the most electrically charged nights the Central Plains has seen.

This Indian sci-fi turns food into power, memory and intergalactic identityAn Indian sci-fi series is turning food into a language of power, identity, and belonging across galaxies. In this immersive conversation, Lavanya Lakshminarayan reveals how Intergalactic Feast expands her bold universe, where memory, migration, and taste collide in ways readers may have never experienced before.

These cities are at high risk from super El Nino set to hit IndiaAround 60% of Indian farmers are entirely dependent on monsoon rainfall for the kharif crop season, making the stakes for this year's rains exceptionally high.

sports.headline: Rahul Dravid disagrees with Gambhir's anti-superstar stance: We need heroes Rahul Dravid has pushed back against Gautam Gambhir's call to end superstar culture in Indian cricket, arguing that every sport needs heroes.

Man charged after Scottish referee put under police watch for Celtic penalty row Police charged a 19-year-old after referee John Beaton's personal details were shared online. The Scottish FA said the case reflects a wider climate of abuse and intimidation around match officials.

ICC meeting in Ahmedabad during IPL final. Will Pak chief Mohsin Naqvi attend? Following the relocation of the ICC meetings to Ahmedabad, PCB Chairman Mohsin Naqvi's travel to India remains highly uncertain despite reports claiming he has been invited to the IPL 2026 final.

Tired of being chased by cameras, Virat Kohli hits out at IPL content culture Virat Kohli has criticised the IPL's growing content culture, saying constant filming disrupts training and private conversations. His remarks underline the tension between fan engagement demands and a player's need for space.

Thailand Open: Satwik-Chirag complete comeback win to enter 1st final of season Thailand Open: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty completed a sensational comeback win over Malaysia's Goh Sze Fei and Nur Izzudin to make it to their first final of the season. The Indian star duo won a three-game thriller to make it to the summit clash of the Thailand Open on Sunday, May 16

KKR vs GT: Will Gujarat, rain perform last rites on KKR's slim playoff hopes? KKR have their playoff hopes hanging by a thread and take on an in-form GT side, aiming to secure their spot in the playoffs. Will Gujarat and potential rain spell end for Kolkata's qualification hopes?

Hockey India removes U-18 team coach over misconduct allegations Hockey India has expelled sub-junior support staff member Sudhir Golla from the national under-18 camp following serious allegations of sexual misconduct and negligence during his training stint in Ranchi.

Kamboj was terrified: Ex-IND captain points out when CSK star lost plot vs LSG Kris Srikkanth said Anshul Kamboj lost control after Mitchell Marsh attacked him early against LSG. He argued CSK misread the moment and should have persisted with their left-arm quicks, Spencer Johnson and Mukesh Choudhary.

Brand Beckham soars: David Beckham becomes UK's first billionaire sportsman David and Victoria Beckham have crossed £1.185 billion on the latest Sunday Times Rich List. The milestone reflects Inter Miami's surge, Victoria's turnaround and Beckham's enduring commercial pull.

They funded their own dreams. Now Netherlands women are ready to play a World Cup The Netherlands are all set to make their Women's T20 World Cup debut next month in England and Wales. In an exclusive conversation with IndiaToday.in, Dutch skipper Babette de Leede reflected on the team's remarkable journey, from once struggling for recognition and resources to now stepping onto cricket's biggest stage.

Pakistan's Fatima Sana scores fastest Women's T20I fifty, smashes Richa's record Fatima Sana smashed a 15-ball fifty as Pakistan piled up 223 for 4 against Zimbabwe in Karachi. The innings broke the Women's T20I record previously held by Richa Ghosh, Sophie Devine and Phoebe Litchfield.

MS Dhoni set for CSK return vs SunRisers in Chennai? Ex-teammate drops big hintIPL 2026: Ravichandran Ashwin has hinted that MS Dhoni could return for Chennai Super Kings against SunRisers Hyderabad in Chennai. The Super Kings need to win their last two games to retain their hopes of moving into the playoffs.

Ain't trending no more: Akash's note celebration vs CSK leaves Steyn fed upAkash Singh flashed a handwritten note after striking against CSK in Lucknow. But Dale Steyn wasn't too pleased with the celebration, claiming that flashing the note was never a trend in the first place.

Not Dhoni's pragmatism: Ruturaj stuns fans with bold take on CSK's playoff chancesIPL 2026, LSG vs CSK: As a crushing defeat to Lucknow left Chennai's playoff hopes on a knife-edge, captain Ruturaj Gaikwad's surprisingly relaxed assessment of a complex qualification path has left the franchise's faithful thoroughly bewildered.

8 sixes in 16 balls: Lack of clarity gets the better of CSK against LucknowChennai Super Kings have put themselves in a precarious position after losing to bottom-placed Lucknow Super Giants. What led to the loss, which now threatens to put them out of the playoffs race? We take a look at the IPL Play of the Day.

CSK qualification scenario: Can Chennai reach playoffs after loss vs LSG?CSK playoffs qualification scenario: Chennai Super Kings' loss to Lucknow Super Giants meant that the team took a huge blow to their playoffs chances. Here's how CSK can still qualify for the IPL 2026 playoffs.

Marsh rides the Lucknow bounce, puts huge dent in CSK's playoffs chancesIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings' playoffs chances became really complicated after the team lost to Lucknow Super Giants on Friday, May 15. Mitchell Marsh blew away the side with a scintillating innings of 90 runs.

High level ICC team reaches Sri Lanka amid leadership crisis in SLCA high-level ICC delegation, including a senior BCCI official, has reached Sri Lanka following the government's appointment of an interim committee to run Sri Lanka Cricket. The visit comes at a sensitive time.

Manchester United set to confirm Michael Carrick as permanent head coachManchester United are close to appointing Michael Carrick as permanent head coach after talks over a two-year deal. The move follows a sharp upturn in results during his interim spell and growing backing from senior club executives.

Thailand Open: Lakshya Sen crashes out after losing to unyielding VitidsarnIndia's top men's singles player Lakshya Sen crashed out of the Thailand Open on Friday, May 15. Playing against Kunlavut Vitidsarn, Lakshya lost in straight games, marking the end of India's campaign in the singles division.

Spencer Johnson replaces Jamie Overton for CSK; LSG make wholesale changesSpencer Johnson made his Chennai Super Kings debut as Ruturaj Gaikwad's side took on Lucknow Super Giants in a crucial IPL 2026 clash in Lucknow on Friday.

Project Bumrah? Ex India star urges Mumbai Indians to look beyond Hardik PandyaIPL 2026: Jasprit Bumrah's calm captaincy stint against Punjab Kings has restarted an old Mumbai Indians debate. Sanjay Manjrekar now believes the franchise may need to start thinking beyond Hardik Pandya.

Eyes on 2036 Olympics: India looking at their pathways to secure hosting rightsA Sports Ministry review of Commonwealth Games preparations also included a discussion on advancing India's 2036 Olympic bid. The conversation reflected planning around Ahmedabad's sporting roadmap even as the IOC process remains frozen.

New Zealand's Rachin Ravindra leaves KKR camp to prepare for England TestsRachin Ravindra has left the Kolkata Knight Riders camp to begin preparations for New Zealand's upcoming Test tour of England. His departure comes at a critical juncture of IPL 2026, with KKR facing a string of must-win matches to keep their playoff hopes alive in an increasingly tight race.

China secures broadcasting deal for FIFA World Cup 2026. Will India follow suit?China has secured a FIFA World Cup broadcast deal, but India is still without a rights holder. The delay has now reached the Delhi High Court as questions grow over public access before kickoff.

Virat Kohli explains why helping IPL's new stars like Prince Yadav is worth itVirat Kohli said he is happy to advise young stars like Prince Yadav during the IPL, even if it comes at the cost of his own wicket during matches. Kohli revealed how he told Prince Yadav on how to dismiss him.

ICC penalises Pakistan with WTC point deduction and fine after Dhaka defeatThe ICC fined Pakistan 40 percent of their match fees and docked eight World Test Championship points for a slow over-rate during the first Test against Bangladesh in Dhaka.

What've you done for Punjab? Arshdeep lands in hot water again after Snapchat rantPunjab Kings pacer Arshdeep Singh came under fresh scrutiny after a Snapchat reply to a fan went viral amid an ongoing controversy surrounding his remarks in IPL-related videos.

CSK, RCB sign replacements for Khaleel Ahmed and Nuwan ThusharaCSK signed domestic pacer Kuldip Yadav for Rs 30 lakh after Khaleel Ahmed suffered a quadriceps tear. Meanwhile, RCB signed Richard Gleeson for Rs 1.6 crore following Nuwan Thushara's withdrawal.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
